pack_purpose: Deterministic narrow follow-up pack for targeted escalation. It defines the crux questions and operational budgets, while source choice may remain model-driven within runtime-enforced tool guardrails.
verification_questions: Does the latest available information materially change the decision, given: BTC can reprice sharply over the weekend and the contract resolves on a Monday noon ET minute close.?
How decision-relevant is the remaining contract/source-of-truth ambiguity: Exact ET-to-Binance minute mapping is clear enough but single-minute settlement mechanics remain operationally sensitive.?
Are the current blockers still material to final judgment: No decisive independently verified volatility/base-rate model for the chance of an 8%+ drawdown by settlement.?
Is the main verification gap still decision-material: No strong independent quantification of six-day downside-tail frequency from a 74k-to-68k starting point was added beyond lane-level reasoning.?

structured_handoff_focus.contract_ambiguity_reason: Exact ET-to-Binance minute mapping is clear enough but single-minute settlement mechanics remain operationally sensitive.
structured_handoff_focus.verification_gap_summary: No strong independent quantification of six-day downside-tail frequency from a 74k-to-68k starting point was added beyond lane-level reasoning.
structured_handoff_focus.best_countercase_summary: An ordinary crypto drawdown or venue-specific bad print into the exact settlement minute can still flip NO despite BTC being comfortably above strike today.
structured_handoff_focus.main_reason_for_disagreement: Remaining disagreement is mostly about how much probability to haircut for minute-specific settlement and crypto downside-tail risk.
structured_handoff_focus.resolution_mechanics_summary: Resolve from Binance BTC/USDT final close of the April 20 12:00 ET one-minute candle; price must be strictly above 68000.
structured_handoff_focus.freshness_driver: BTC can reprice sharply over the weekend and the contract resolves on a Monday noon ET minute close.
decision_blockers: No decisive independently verified volatility/base-rate model for the chance of an 8%+ drawdown by settlement.
Single-minute Binance settlement leaves residual venue and microstructure fragility.
Any fresh weekend or Monday-morning macro/crypto shock could materially change the estimate.

supporting_persona_summaries.main_thesis: Market is directionally right that >68k is likely, but 93.5-94% looks somewhat overconfident given six-day BTC volatility and the exact Binance noon-ET minute-close condition.
main_logical_chain: Market implies about 93.5-94% Yes because current Binance spot is comfortably above 68k.
Direct Binance data supports a strong Yes lean because recent spot and range have stayed above the strike.
But the contract is narrow and short-horizon crypto downside tails are not negligible.
So the correct update is high-probability Yes, but modestly below market at 88%.
strongest_supports: Binance BTC/USDT traded around 74.2k during the run, about 9.2% above the 68k strike.
Recent Binance daily lows in the sampled week stayed above roughly 70.5k.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC can still move 8-10% in six days.
Resolution depends on one exact Binance 1-minute close at noon ET, adding timing and venue-specific risk.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact short-horizon downside-tail frequency was not quantified in a formal base-rate study.
Noon-ET candle operational mapping appears clear from rules but still matters mechanically.
supporting_persona_summaries.what_would_change_view: A move of Binance BTC/USDT back near 70k, a new macro/crypto shock, or evidence of Binance-specific settlement noise would lower the estimate; continued stable trading above 72k would raise it.
supporting_persona_summaries.timing_relevance: High: the market resolves on April 20, 2026 at the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close, so date/time precision is material.
supporting_persona_summaries.source_quality_view: Primary contract source and settlement venue data are strong, but evidence independence is only medium and no authoritative future-settling source exists yet.
